jokes.text: Please, Will, the last time you showed me a magic trick... it took two months for my hair to grow back.
jokes.setup: Will wants to show Ashley another magic trick
jokes.punchline: His previous magic attempt was so disastrous it burned off her hair
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor that establishes Will's incompetence through exaggerated consequences. Good economy and believable sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: What happened to 'abracadabra'? / No, this is black magic, Ash.
jokes.setup: Ashley questions Will's unconventional magic words
jokes.punchline: Will's racially-aware twist on traditional magic terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Will's personality while subverting magic show expectations. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Chaka Khan!
jokes.setup: Will performing magic with theatrical flair
jokes.punchline: Uses funk singer's name as magic words instead of traditional incantations
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration. Will's street culture bleeding into upper-class magic performance is quintessential Fresh Prince humor.

jokes.text: I don't have a dollar. / I have a 20. / That'll do.
jokes.setup: Will needs a dollar for his trick
jokes.punchline: Ashley casually offers a $20 bill, highlighting the family's wealth disparity with Will's expectations
jokes.explanation: Classic wealth/class humor that defines the show's premise. Will's quick acceptance adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Going... going... gone! Will!
jokes.setup: Will dramatically building up to making the $20 disappear
jokes.punchline: The money actually disappears (presumably into his pocket), Ashley realizes she's been conned
jokes.explanation: Predictable but functional physical comedy. The setup is clear and the payoff works, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Some nice twelve-grain organic... hot cereal.
jokes.setup: Vivian presenting breakfast with enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: The overly health-conscious description reveals her obsessive new lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor establishing Vivian's new health obsession. Sets up later jokes but isn't particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Boy, that was better than the batch you made yesterday. / Good. That's why I made twice as much today.
jokes.setup: Carlton politely compliments the improved cereal
jokes.punchline: Vivian's response reveals she's inflicting more of her bad cooking on them
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - Carlton's diplomatic compliment backfires. Shows the family's polite suffering.

jokes.text: You keep dishing it up, Aunt Viv... and we'll keep finding some place to put it.
jokes.setup: Will responding to Vivian's cooking enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Double entendre suggesting they'll dispose of rather than eat her food
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with Will's diplomatic yet sarcastic delivery. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: She picks our clothes out for us... and checks our homework and cuts the crust off the end of our bread.
jokes.setup: Hillary lists nice things about mom being home
jokes.punchline: The activities get increasingly infantilizing for teenagers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how overbearing Aunt Viv has become

jokes.text: Hey, look, that woman must be stopped, y'all.
jokes.setup: Family members expressing mixed feelings about Vivian being home
jokes.punchline: Will's direct, street-smart assessment cuts through their politeness
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's running gag with Will's characteristic directness. Becomes a quotable catchphrase.

jokes.text: That diet and exercise regimen your mother put me on... has really changed my life. I never felt better.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil talks about Aunt Viv's health regimen
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm is immediately undercut by what comes next
jokes.explanation: Sets up the expectation before the punchline that follows

jokes.text: That woman must be stopped.
jokes.setup: Philip initially defending Vivian's new lifestyle
jokes.punchline: He adopts Will's exact phrase, showing he's reached his breaking point too
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows character development. Philip's proper demeanor cracking to use Will's street phrase is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Is that G or Philip Michael Thomas?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey appears in a flashy outfit Vivian made him wear
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to Miami Vice actor, mocking the inappropriate styling
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that highlights the visual comedy of Geoffrey's outfit. Time-period specific but effective.

jokes.text: That woman must be stopped!
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explaining his outfit situation
jokes.punchline: Even the usually composed butler adopts Will's rallying cry
jokes.explanation: Strong callback escalation. Geoffrey joining the chorus adds to the running gag's momentum.

jokes.text: My personal favorite was George Washington and the Hussy.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Sonya's historical dramas
jokes.punchline: Fake movie title combining founding father with suggestive word
jokes.explanation: Clever fake movie title that establishes the pattern of Sonya's B-movie career

jokes.text: Ladies and gentleman, give it up for the star of Heidi Comes to Harlem.
jokes.setup: Will introducing Sonya Lamor
jokes.punchline: Made-up movie title combining innocent children's story with urban setting
jokes.explanation: Creative fake movie title that's both absurd and perfectly fitting for a B-movie actress. Good character voice for Will.

jokes.text: Mom, if you make me go to school like this... I'm going to write a book when I grow up.
jokes.setup: Ashley forced to wear an embarrassing outfit to school
jokes.punchline: Her threat is to write a tell-all book rather than typical childhood rebellion
jokes.explanation: Smart kid humor that subverts expected threats. Ashley's precocious response fits her character well.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, that was for you. They said you left your wallet at the Donut Depot.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil is talking about his healthy diet
jokes.punchline: Will reveals Phil was caught at a donut shop
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character moment - exposes Uncle Phil's diet cheating right after his health speech

jokes.text: Pretty fly earmuffs you got there, Ash.
jokes.setup: Ashley appears wearing something on her head
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically calls her hair accessories 'earmuffs'
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with Will's signature sarcasm. Relies on the absurdity of Ashley's appearance.

jokes.text: A little calamine lotion probably'd take care of that rash.
jokes.setup: Continuing to mock Ashley's appearance
jokes.punchline: Compares her outfit/hair to a skin condition needing medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the visual gag, getting more specific with the medical reference.

jokes.text: Come on, Ash, not many women can carry off plaid. / And here comes one now.
jokes.setup: Will seemingly defending Ashley's plaid outfit
jokes.punchline: Hilary enters also wearing plaid, revealing Will was setting up a joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection setup-punchline structure. Will appears to be complimenting but sets up the visual punchline perfectly.

jokes.text: Don't worry about this, Ash. One day you're gonna be cracking up laughing about this with your therapist.
jokes.setup: Will comforting Ashley about her embarrassing outfit
jokes.punchline: Suggests this trauma will require therapy, implying lasting psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's still family-friendly. Great subversion of typical comfort, very quotable line.

jokes.text: I wanna play!
jokes.setup: Family members dressed identically in plaid by Vivian
jokes.punchline: Hilary's childish enthusiasm about joining the matching outfit situation
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate humor for Hilary's vapid personality, though not particularly strong on its own.

jokes.text: Look at poor Henry Firth. Ever since his wife left him he's been in one meaningless... unsatisfying relationship after another.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv points out a lonely divorced man at the party
jokes.punchline: She describes his dating life with obvious pity
jokes.explanation: Sets up what becomes a running gag about Henry Firth but isn't particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: That is so kind of you. Please, no pictures.
jokes.setup: Sonya arrives and people are excited to see her
jokes.punchline: She acts like paparazzi are present when there are none
jokes.explanation: Establishes Sonya's delusional star behavior - becomes her catchphrase

jokes.text: You look dope in 3-D! / It wasn't in 3-D. / Back up.
jokes.setup: Will complimenting Sonya's movie performance
jokes.punchline: His compliment is based on a technical effect that didn't exist, showing his ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's enthusiastic but uninformed fan behavior. Sharp dialogue structure.

jokes.text: There is something that I am just dying to know. / Yes, I did it. And he deserved to be slapped.
jokes.setup: Vivian building up to ask a question
jokes.punchline: Sonya assumes it's about a scandal and immediately confesses
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke. Sonya's defensive response reveals more than intended, good character comedy.

jokes.text: When Jacoby sued Meyers, who do you think he called?
jokes.setup: Henry is trying to impress Sonya with Phil's legal prowess
jokes.punchline: References an obscure legal case that means nothing to anyone
jokes.explanation: Shows Henry's awkward attempts at small talk with legal insider information

jokes.text: I couldn't eat a thing. Wonderful sugar cake. Can I get a little, tiny, weensy piece, please?
jokes.setup: Sonya claims she couldn't eat anything
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself by asking for cake
jokes.explanation: Classic character hypocrisy - says one thing, immediately does the opposite

jokes.text: Will, check the pool, the Jacuzzi, the tennis courts, and the basketball net.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil just said Will shouldn't check those places
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv immediately tells him to check exactly those places
jokes.explanation: Great marital dynamic - Aunt Viv undermines Uncle Phil's parenting immediately

jokes.text: But I did find Geoffrey's new uniform crammed behind the hot-water heater.
jokes.setup: Carlton is reporting on his search for the silver
jokes.punchline: He found Geoffrey's uniform hidden in a bizarre location
jokes.explanation: Adds to the mystery with absurd hiding place, but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: We need that little white lady from Murder, She Wrote.
jokes.setup: They're trying to solve the mystery of missing silver
jokes.punchline: Will references Jessica Fletcher as the ideal detective for their situation
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits the mystery situation, Will's perspective

jokes.text: And if you all happen to be digging around under my bed... those videotapes are for my report on Clarence Thomas.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions searching under his bed
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explains away potentially embarrassing videos with weak excuse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Uncle Phil's transparent attempt to explain away something embarrassing

jokes.text: So kind of you. No pictures.
jokes.setup: Sonya answers her door
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivers her catchphrase to Will and Carlton
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation - less funny each time but establishes character consistency

jokes.text: Laurence Olivier was in An Officer and a Hussy? No it was his brother, Wendall.
jokes.setup: Will is impressed that Sonya worked with Laurence Olivier
jokes.punchline: She reveals it was his fictional brother Wendall
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - deflates prestige by revealing it's a fake relative

jokes.text: You got top billing over Clark Gable? / It was Schlomo Gable, his half-brother.
jokes.setup: Carlton impressed by Sonya's apparent star power
jokes.punchline: Reveals it wasn't the famous Clark Gable but a made-up relative
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist humor with perfect name choice. 'Schlomo Gable' is inherently funny and deflates Carlton's admiration.

jokes.text: Then I guess this Reagan isn't really Ronald. / No, that is really Ronald. You see, we couldn't get his brother.
jokes.setup: Will testing the pattern of fake celebrity relatives
jokes.punchline: Sonya reveals this time it actually WAS the real celebrity, but only because the fake one wasn't available
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that subverts expectations twice. The logic is perfectly absurd - they got the real Reagan by accident.

jokes.text: Then I guess this Reagan isn't really Ronald. No, that is really Ronald. You see, we couldn't get his brother.
jokes.setup: Will expects another fake celebrity relative
jokes.punchline: This time it actually IS the real celebrity, but for a pathetic reason
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverts the pattern but makes it even more pathetic

jokes.text: What's up, man? I'm Will. Polly want a cracker? / Will is a cracker. / Yeah, man, your mother poops on papers.
jokes.setup: Will greeting Sonya's parrot with typical phrase
jokes.punchline: Parrot insults Will, then Will childishly insults the parrot back
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will getting into an argument with a bird. The escalation to juvenile insults is perfect.

jokes.text: This is not a wimpy fork. This is a hand-engraved silver dessert... Jumping Jehosaphat!
jokes.setup: Carlton defends the fork's quality
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence he realizes it's his mother's stolen silverware
jokes.explanation: Great discovery moment with perfect timing - Carlton's realization mid-description

jokes.text: This is not a wimpy fork. This is a hand-engraved silver dessert.... Jumping Jehosaphat!
jokes.setup: Carlton defending the fork's quality with typical pompousness
jokes.punchline: His dramatic reaction when he realizes it's his mother's stolen silverware
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his snobbery interrupted by shock. 'Jumping Jehosaphat' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Remember what happened to her in Hussy Behind Bars?
jokes.setup: Carlton wants to call the police on Sonya
jokes.punchline: Will references another fake B-movie to argue against it
jokes.explanation: Continues the fake movie title pattern while advancing the plot

jokes.text: I say we do what's fair and just for both parties. Steal it back and run like hell.
jokes.setup: Will proposing a diplomatic solution to the silverware theft
jokes.punchline: His 'fair and just' solution is actually more theft and fleeing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Will voice. The formal setup contrasting with criminal solution is brilliantly executed.

jokes.text: Carlton, weren't you just saying how much... you would enjoy a tour of Ms. Lamor's house? No...time like the present.
jokes.setup: Will needs to create a distraction
jokes.punchline: Pretends Carlton expressed interest in something he obviously didn't
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the heist but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Get your hands off that! Not till we're married.
jokes.setup: Carlton being shown around Sonya's house during the heist
jokes.punchline: Carlton's prudish reaction to presumably touching something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Carlton's conservative values even during a crime. Physical comedy implied.

jokes.text: Is the silver in your pockets? / No, I'm just extremely happy to see you.
jokes.setup: Will checking if Carlton successfully stole the silverware
jokes.punchline: Carlton's response plays on the classic Mae West innuendo line
jokes.explanation: Classic reference delivered by Carlton, whose innocent delivery makes the innuendo funnier. Good timing.

jokes.text: Is the silver in your pockets? No, I'm just extremely happy to see you.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Carlton has hidden the stolen silverware
jokes.punchline: Carlton's response has unintended sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Mae West reference but feels forced and inappropriate for Carlton's character

jokes.text: Carlton, please, we lifted some silverware from a neighbor... and I had to slap you twice to keep you from driving us to Mexico.
jokes.setup: Carlton bragging about his criminal prowess
jokes.punchline: Will deflates him by revealing Carlton's panic and dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Carlton's delusions of criminal competence vs. reality. The Mexico detail is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I just thought we should lay low until the heat was off.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to defend his Mexico escape plan
jokes.punchline: Uses crime movie clichés for a simple silverware theft
jokes.explanation: Carlton continues treating minor theft like major crime with movie clichés

jokes.text: The caterers brought it back.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks the silver was stolen
jokes.punchline: It was just borrowed by caterers - completely innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Good twist that makes all their criminal planning unnecessary

jokes.text: Carlton, why do I have service for eight in my drawers?
jokes.setup: Family discovering the missing silver was returned by caterers
jokes.punchline: Vivian's matter-of-fact question about the excess silverware Carlton stole
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment that exposes the boys' mistake. Vivian's calm delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We'll get caught. We'll go to jail. Kid 'N Play will portray us in a movie.
jokes.setup: Carlton panicking about having to break back into Sonya's house
jokes.punchline: His fears escalate to being portrayed by a contemporary rap duo in a prison movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific escalation that captures Carlton's dramatic fears. Kid 'N Play reference is perfect for the era and Carlton's character.

jokes.text: No, I meant everyone always mistakes me for Whitney Houston.
jokes.setup: Hilary thinking they'll be surrounded by autograph seekers
jokes.punchline: Reveals she thinks she looks like Whitney Houston, showing her delusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her delusional self-image. The Whitney Houston comparison is absurd enough to be funny.

jokes.text: Will, there's a big black guy in the house.
jokes.setup: Carlton and Will breaking back into Sonya's house
jokes.punchline: Carlton's panicked description of Will himself as a threat
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Carlton's panicked state causing him to not recognize Will. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Will, there's a big black guy in the house.
jokes.setup: Carlton is supposed to be the lookout during their break-in
jokes.punchline: He's alerting to Will himself, showing his panic and confusion
jokes.explanation: Great panic joke - Carlton is so nervous he doesn't recognize his own partner

jokes.text: I brought these gloves so we don't leave prints. Yellow or blue?
jokes.setup: Carlton is trying to be professional about their crime
jokes.punchline: He's worried about fashion choices for their rubber gloves
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - even during crimes he's concerned with aesthetic choices

jokes.text: If Meryl Streep is going to play it, she must have her nose fixed. Absolutely. Page 3, the schnoz clause.
jokes.setup: Sonya discusses casting for her movie
jokes.punchline: She has specific contractual demands about Meryl Streep's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - washed-up actress making demands about A-list star

jokes.text: When we walk down the street, deer eat from our hand.
jokes.setup: Philip describing his happy marriage to Sonya
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd metaphor for marital bliss involving Disney-like animal behavior
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist line that's become iconic. Philip's earnest delivery of completely ridiculous imagery is perfect.

jokes.text: Sonya, with all due respect... you're the one who's playing footsie-wootsie.
jokes.setup: Sonya accuses Uncle Phil of inappropriate contact
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly defends himself using childish terminology
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's dignified character using silly words creates mild humor

jokes.text: Please, darlings, you are too kind. No pictures.
jokes.setup: Everyone discovers Will and Carlton under the table
jokes.punchline: Sonya's automatic response to the chaos
jokes.explanation: Running gag continues even in crisis - shows character's automatic behavior

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, I know what you're thinking, and there's no cake left.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil discovers them in a compromising situation
jokes.punchline: Will assumes he's thinking about food instead of their obvious crime
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's knowledge of Uncle Phil's priorities

jokes.text: Will made me do it. He held a fork to my head.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to explain their criminal behavior to Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Claims Will threatened him with the least threatening weapon possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton panic response. The image of being threatened with a fork is absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And I didn't want to hide under the furniture... like a bad old episode of Laverne & Shirley.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing all the things he didn't want to do during their scheme
jokes.punchline: Compares their sitcom situation to another sitcom, breaking the fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor acknowledging sitcom conventions. Carlton's specific reference shows his TV knowledge and the absurdity of their situation.
